text:
LA County Public Health - Maternal, Child, & Adolescent Health
CPSP Provider Information and Resources
Who Can Be
								A CPSP Provider?
Any physician, certified nurse midwife, hospital, community clinic, or medical group who
provides prenatal care
,
has a valid Medi-Cal provider
									number
, and
is in good standing with state licensing and regulatory
									agencies
.
Provider
								Enrollment
Medi-Cal Managed Care Providers must enroll with Medi-Cal and have each clinic site
								enrolled. An application and letter of approval is not needed to begin
CPSP or
									CPSP-like services
.
According to the
2024 Managed Care Boilerplate Contract
, which can be
								found
here
, you will see on page 367 that the contract
								requires providers to use the most current standards of ACOG and
CPSP
.
For questions or confirmation regarding enrollment, contact your Managed Care Plan or
								DHCS via email at
dhcsmedicalpolicy@dhcs.ca.gov
.
Reimbursement for CPSP Services
Any physician, certified nurse midwife, hospital, community clinic, or medical group who
provides prenatal care
,
has a valid Medi-Cal provider
									number
, and
is in good standing with state licensing and regulatory
									agencies
.
Billing Questions?
Contact your
Managed Care Plan
or
Medi-Cal
:
Call Us
Email Us
For more information, visit:
Medi-Cal Outreach & Education Website
